deep_dive.title: Large Language Model-Driven Cooperative Operator Ensemble Evolution for Permutation Flow Shop Scheduling
deep_dive.abstract: The permutation flow shop scheduling problem (PFSP) is a classical NP-hard combinatorial optimization problem in intelligent manufacturing. In practice, PFSP is commonly addressed using metaheuristic algorithms, among which the iterated greedy (IG) algorithm is widely adopted due to its simplicity and strong empirical performance. However, classical IG relies on a single fixed destruction operator, which often limits exploration and leads to search stagnation on large and complex problem instances. To address this issue, this work proposes a multi-operator IG algorithm, termed IG-DOE, which enhances exploration by switching among heterogeneous destruction operators along a single search trajectory. The core mechanism, called stagnation-triggered sequential switching, activates the next destruction operator in an ordered destruction operator ensemble (DOE) when stagnation is detected, thereby enriching the perturbation behavior of classical IG. Moreover, to reduce reliance on expert-crafted operators, a large language model (LLM)-assisted framework, termed SCOE, is introduced to automatically construct a high-quality DOE through stagewise evolution, state-awareness, and cooperative evaluation. Experiments on the challenging \textit{VRF-hard-large} benchmark show that the DOE evolved from smaller problem instances generalizes well to larger unseen instances. Under the same CPU-time limit, IG-DOE obtained much better average performance than QIG, a state-of-the-art IG algorithm. Additional experiments on real-world industrial-data-derived instances further show that the evolved DOE can generalize effectively to different data distributions without additional adaptation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-driven cooperative operator ensemble evolution for scheduling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 大语言模型驱动的合作算子集成进化用于调度
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 大语言模型驱动的合作算子集成进化用于调度
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 大语言模型驱动的合作算子集成进化用于调度
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Large language model dynamically controls selection and combination of destruction operators in iterated greedy algorithm, enhancing exploration for permutation flow shop scheduling problems.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对置换流水车间调度问题，经典迭代贪心算法因单一破坏算子导致搜索停滞。本文提出IG-DOE，利用大语言模型在单条搜索轨迹中动态切换异构破坏算子，形成一个多算子集成进化机制。实验结果表明，该策略显著提升了算法在大规模复杂实例上的探索能力和求解质量。该工作展示了LLM与进化计算结合在自动发现高效算子组合方面的潜力。

deep_dive.title: Large Language Model-Driven Cooperative Operator Ensemble Evolution for Permutation Flow Shop Scheduling
deep_dive.abstract: The permutation flow shop scheduling problem (PFSP) is a classical NP-hard combinatorial optimization problem in intelligent manufacturing. In practice, PFSP is commonly addressed using metaheuristic algorithms, among which the iterated greedy (IG) algorithm is widely adopted due to its simplicity and strong empirical performance. However, classical IG relies on a single fixed destruction operator, which often limits exploration and leads to search stagnation on large and complex problem instances. The core mechanism, called stagnation-triggered sequential switching, activates the next destruction operator in an ordered destruction operator ensemble (DOE) when stagnation is detected, thereby enriching the perturbation behavior of classical IG. Moreover, to reduce reliance on expert-crafted operators, a large language model (LLM)-assisted framework, termed SCOE, is introduced to automatically construct a high-quality DOE through stagewise evolution, state-awareness, and cooperative evaluation. Experiments on the challenging VRF-hard-large benchmark show that the DOE evolved from smaller problem instances generalizes well to larger unseen instances. deep_dive.title: Interpreting Neural Combinatorial Optimization via Evolving Programmatic Bottlenecks
deep_dive.abstract: Neural Combinatorial Optimization (NCO) achieves strong performance, yet its black-box nature remains a key roadblock to deployment and scientific diagnosis. Standard interpretability tools, such as Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs), are ill-equipped for NCO, whose decisions are dynamic, state-dependent, and lack proper concept vocabulary definition. To close this gap, we introduce Evolving Programmatic Bottlenecks (EPB), to our knowledge, the first framework for interpreting NCO policies by distilling black-box NCO models into human-readable program portfolios. EPB employs an LLM to autonomously evolve a bank of programs, where each program's per-step action distribution serves as the bottleneck. EPB works through an iterative framework: Block I fixes program bank capacity and introduces a hybrid textual-numerical gradient descent scheme that couples numerical gradients for student router updates and textual gradients for LLM-based program revision; Block II dynamically adapts bank capacity via fault-targeted expansion and redundancy pruning. Extensive experiments demonstrate EPB's effectiveness and broad applicability, where the distilled program portfolios largely match original performance. EPB also reveals that NCO behavior shifts across optimization stages and can be approximated as a composition of classic heuristic variants. Our work advances interpretable NCO and establishes EPB as a promising tool for interpreting sequential decision-making models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM evolves programmatic bottlenecks for interpreting neural combinatorial optimization, combining LLM, evolutionary computation, and automatic discovery
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用LLM自动演化程序瓶颈来解释神经组合优化策略，结合LLM、进化计算和自动发现
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用LLM自动演化程序瓶颈来解释神经组合优化策略，结合LLM、进化计算和自动发现
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用LLM自动演化程序瓶颈来解释神经组合优化策略，结合LLM、进化计算和自动发现
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes EPB framework that uses an LLM to autonomously evolve human-readable program portfolios as interpretable bottlenecks for black-box NCO policies, enabling algorithmic discovery through iterative program evolution.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 神经组合优化（NCO）虽然性能出色但缺乏可解释性。本文提出演化程序瓶颈（EPB）框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）自动演化出一组可读程序作为瓶颈，将黑盒NCO策略蒸馏成人类可理解的程序集合。EPB通过迭代循环使LLM不断改进程序库，每个程序的动作分布作为瓶颈表示。在多个组合优化任务上的实验表明，EPB不仅提供了有意义的解释，还能揭示策略的潜在决策逻辑。该工作为结合LLM和进化计算进行自动算法发现开辟了新途径。

deep_dive.title: RACL: Reasoning-Agent Control Layers for Continuous Metaheuristic Learning
deep_dive.abstract: This paper introduces RACL, a Reasoning-Agent Control Layer for metaheuristics. RACL places a reasoning agent above an existing optimizer. The agent does not replace the optimizer and does not modify business constraints. Instead, it controls the optimizer's internal search behavior by observing operational memory, reasoning over past behavior, formulating bounded hypotheses, testing interventions, evaluating outcomes, applying guardrails, consolidating useful policies and explaining its decisions. The experiment uses vehicle routing as a testbed, but the contribution is not a new routing solver, a particular ALNS configuration or a specific set of routing rules. The contribution is the RACL method: a way for a reasoning agent to discover, validate, consolidate and explain algorithmic control rules for a metaheuristic. In the current experimental setting, RACL improves or ties the Operational Memory Policy in 21 of 21 feasible cases and improves or ties a non-reasoning Stagnation-Triggered Policy in 18 of 21 feasible cases, with an average RACL vs STP cost delta of -0.641%. In the Sevilla-9/10 runtime sample, RACL improves average cost by -8.337% versus Fixed and -1.605% versus STP without showing material computational overhead. During the proof-of-concept, Codex was used as an in-the-loop reasoning agent observing executions, interpreting logs and proposing live bounded interventions. The policy proxy was later used only to make quantitative evaluation reproducible.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: control layer for metaheuristics to automatically discover policies
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 控制层用于元启发式以自动发现策略
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 控制层用于元启发式以自动发现策略
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 控制层用于元启发式以自动发现策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: RACL places a reasoning agent above an existing optimizer to observe, hypothesize, and test interventions, enabling automatic discovery of effective search policies for combinatorial optimization problems.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出RACL，一个放置在现有优化器之上的推理代理控制层。该代理通过观察操作内存、推理过去行为、假设边界、测试干预、评估结果、应用防护栏、巩固有用策略并解释其决策来动态控制优化器的搜索行为。在车辆路径问题上的实验表明，RACL可以自动发现有效的搜索策略，而无需修改业务约束。该方法为元启发式算法提供了一种持续学习和自动改进的框架。

deep_dive.title: Two-Phase Bilevel Search for the Moving-Target Traveling Salesman Problem with Moving Obstacles
deep_dive.abstract: The Moving-Target Traveling Salesman Problem (MT-TSP) seeks a minimum cost trajectory for an agent that departs from a static depot, visits a set of moving targets, each within one of their assigned time windows, and returns to the depot. In this article, we study the Moving-Target Traveling Salesman Problem with Moving Obstacles (MT-TSP-MO), a generalization of the MT-TSP where the agent trajectory must avoid moving obstacles. We present a Mixed-Integer Conic Programming (MICP) formulation that can be solved using off-the-shelf solvers, as well as a fast and scalable Two-Phase Bilevel Search (TPBS) algorithm that computes high-quality feasible solutions for the problem. We evaluate our approaches against an existing baseline algorithm on a broad range of problem instances with up to 40 targets and 40 obstacles. The results demonstrate that both the proposed methods significantly outperform the baseline with respect to success rates, solution costs, and computation time.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: moving-target TSP with obstacles, heuristic bilevel search algorithm
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 移动目标TSP带障碍物，启发式双层搜索算法

deep_dive.title: Rescaling MLM-Head for Neural Sparse Retrieval
deep_dive.abstract: Learned sparse retrieval (LSR) models such as SPLADE have traditionally used BERT-style masked language models as backbone encoders. A natural expectation is that replacing BERT with stronger pretrained encoders should improve retrieval effectiveness. However, we find that under standard SPLADE training recipes, backbones with large MLM-head L2 norms can suffer performance degradation and even training collapse under standard SPLADE training recipes. We identify this failure as a scale mismatch in the MLM head: SPLADE directly uses MLM-head outputs to construct sparse lexical representations, and query-document relevance is computed by an unnormalized dot product over these representations. As a result, an inflated MLM-head scale can amplify sparse activations, distort matching scores, and destabilize contrastive training under common training settings. To address this issue, we introduce a simple initialization-time correction that rescales the MLM-head projection by a constant factor before SPLADE training. This zero-cost adjustment improves training stability without modifying the model architecture or training objective. Across both in-domain and out-of-domain retrieval benchmarks, this simple correction substantially improves large-norm backbones such as ModernBERT and Ettin, turning unstable training runs into competitive sparse retrievers. In several settings, the corrected models further match or surpass the classic BERT-SPLADE baseline. These findings suggest that the bottleneck in adapting pretrained encoders to LSR is not encoder capacity alone, but the calibration of the MLM-head scale used to construct sparse lexical representations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes rescaling method for MLM-head in neural sparse retrieval, a reranker training technique
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出神经稀疏检索中MLM头的重新缩放方法，属于重排序器训练技术
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出神经稀疏检索中MLM头的重新缩放方法，属于重排序器训练技术
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出神经稀疏检索中MLM头的重新缩放方法，属于重排序器训练技术
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Rescaling MLM-head prevents training collapse in SPLADE-style sparse retrieval when using large-norm backbones.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文发现标准SPLADE训练中，强骨干编码器的MLM头L2范数过大会导致性能下降甚至崩溃。根源在于MLM头输出尺度与稀疏表示的匹配问题。提出一种重缩放方法以稳定训练，使更强骨干能提升检索效果。为神经稀疏检索的重排序器训练提供了实用技巧。

quick_skim.title: DYNA : Dynamic Episodic Memory Networks for Augmenting Large Language Models with Temporal Knowledge Graphs in Continuous Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) struggle to incorporate new knowledge without forgetting or costly retraining. We propose DYNA, a lightweight framework that augments a frozen LLM with a temporal knowledge graph where events are nodes and temporal relations are directed, timestamped edges. The graph serves as an external, updatable memory. At query time, DYNA retrieves relevant nodes via random walks and centrality measures, then augments the LLM's response. Evaluated on three temporal recall tasks, DYNA reduces catastrophic forgetting by ~7% compared to fine-tuning and improves temporal ordering by ~5% over standard RAG. Higher graph clustering coefficients correlate with better retrieval, showing that graph structure matters. Contributions: (1) episodic memory as temporal KG, (2) retraining-free LLM augmentation, (3) graph properties as predictors of retrieval performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: augments LLM with temporal knowledge graph for continuous learning, supports knowledge discovery
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用时间知识图谱增强LLM以支持持续学习和知识发现
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用时间知识图谱增强LLM以支持持续学习和知识发现
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用时间知识图谱增强LLM以支持持续学习和知识发现
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DYNA augments frozen LLM with temporal knowledge graph via dynamic episodic memory, reducing forgetting and improving temporal recall, enabling better knowledge retrieval for discovery.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大型语言模型在持续获取新知识时面临灾难性遗忘或高昂重训成本，限制了其在动态知识发现中的应用。本文提出DYNA，通过构建时间知识图谱作为外部可更新记忆，在查询时利用随机游走和中心性度量检索相关事件节点，并增强LLM响应。在三个时间回忆任务上，DYNA相比微调减少约7%的遗忘，相比标准RAG提升时序顺序约5%。该轻量框架为LLM持续知识更新提供有效方案，有助于科学发现中的知识探索与整合。

quick_skim.title: Integrating Reasoning and Generalization in Text-to-SQL via Self-Enhanced Fine-Tuning
quick_skim.abstract: Text-to-SQL aims to translate natural language questions into executable SQL queries over structured databases, enabling non-expert users to access data intuitively. While recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in this task, existing LLM-based approaches often struggle to strike a balance between strong reasoning capabilities and robust generalization. To address these limitations, we propose CoTE-SQL to enhance the LLM-based text-to-SQL generation with three key innovations: (i) self-enhanced reasoning traces distilled from LLMs without human annotation, (ii) structured chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting with modular decomposition and examples retrieval, and (iii) error-aware revision based on SQL execution feedback. Extensive experiments on the Spider and Bird benchmarks demonstrate that CoTE-SQL achieves new state-of-the-art performance among methods built on open-source LLMs with comparable model sizes on Bird (53.39% EX / 59.02 VES) and strong results on Spider (79.60% EX / 77.19 VES), with especially significant gains on complex queries. Results highlight the effectiveness of combining self-enhancement, structured reasoning, and execution-time feedback within an LLM-based framework for text-to-SQL design.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based text-to-SQL with chain-of-thought reasoning for automated query generation, aiding data discovery
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的Text-to-SQL方法，利用链式思维推理生成查询，辅助数据发现
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于LLM的Text-to-SQL方法，利用链式思维推理生成查询，辅助数据发现
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM的Text-to-SQL方法，利用链式思维推理生成查询，辅助数据发现
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CoTE-SQL, which enhances LLM-based text-to-SQL with self-enhanced reasoning traces, structured CoT, and error-aware revision, improving reasoning and generalization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 将自然语言转化为SQL查询是非专业用户访问数据库的关键，但现有LLM方法在推理能力与泛化性能上难以兼得。本文提出CoTE-SQL，通过自增强推理轨迹（从LLM蒸馏获取）、结构化链式思维提示（包含模块分解和示例检索）以及基于SQL执行结果的错误感知修订，提升了LLM的Text-to-SQL生成效果。在多个基准数据集上，CoTE-SQL优于基线方法，成功平衡了推理与泛化。该工作展示了LLM在自动化数据发现中的巨大潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 将自然语言转化为SQL查询是非专业用户访问数据库的关键，但现有LLM方法在推理能力与泛化性能上难以兼得。本文提出CoTE-SQL，通过自增强推理轨迹（从LLM蒸馏获取）、结构化链式思维提示（包含模块分解和示例检索）以及基于SQL执行结果的错误感知修订，提升了LLM的Text-to-SQL生成效果。在多个基准数据集上，CoTE-SQL优于基线方法，成功平衡了推理与泛化。该工作展示了LLM在自动化数据发现中的巨大潜力。

quick_skim.title: Runtime Analysis of Cartesian Genetic Programming in Evolving Boolean Functions
quick_skim.abstract: Cartesian Genetic Programming (CGP) is among the practical and popular forms of Genetic Programming as it uses a graph-based representation of programs. This paper presents a first runtime analysis of CGP in evolving Boolean functions using complete training sets. We prove an asymptotic bound $O(n D^5)$ for the expected number of fitness evaluations of CGP to construct a conjunction of $n$ inputs using at most $D \geq n-1$ binary gates, a minimal function set, and even with a strict survival selection. When the non-strict selection is used, the bound is improved to $O(n D^4)$. Our analysis reveals interesting characteristics of CGP induced search, which have been only observed empirically. In particular, enabling the acceptance of equally good solutions, including those with connected gates non-contributing to fitness, can lead to a speedup, and consequently a better asymptotic time bound. In contrast to conjunctions, we also prove a negative result which shows that CGP requires exponential time to evolve an exclusive disjunction. Experiments evolving conjunctions complement our theoretical findings. The use of incomplete training sets is found to further reduce the average number of fitness evaluations while maintaining a good level of generalisation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: runtime analysis of Cartesian Genetic Programming, a heuristic evolutionary method
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 笛卡尔遗传编程（一种启发式进化方法）的运行时分析
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 笛卡尔遗传编程（一种启发式进化方法）的运行时分析
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 笛卡尔遗传编程（一种启发式进化方法）的运行时分析
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: First runtime analysis of CGP for evolving Boolean functions, proving expected fitness evaluation bounds under different selection strategies.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 笛卡尔遗传编程（CGP）是一种实用的遗传编程形式，但缺乏运行时分析。本文首次对CGP进化布尔函数进行了期望运行时间分析，证明了在严格选择和非严格选择下的渐近界限，揭示了CGP搜索特性的理论依据。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 笛卡尔遗传编程（CGP）是一种实用的遗传编程形式，但缺乏运行时分析。本文首次对CGP进化布尔函数进行了期望运行时间分析，证明了在严格选择和非严格选择下的渐近界限，揭示了CGP搜索特性的理论依据。

quick_skim.title: Understanding the Behaviors of Environment-aware Information Retrieval
quick_skim.abstract: Recent retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) approaches have demonstrated strong capability in handling complex queries, yet current research overlooks a critical challenge: different retrievers require fundamentally different query formulation strategies for optimal performance. In this work, we present the first systematic analysis of how LLMs can learn to adapt their query formulation strategies for different retrievers via reinforcement learning (RL). Our empirical study reveals that RL effectively teaches an LLM to tailor its queries to specific retriever characteristics. We discover that different retrievers exhibit surprisingly distinct optimal query styles (e.g., descriptive vs. question-like), suggesting strategies learned for one retriever ineffective for another. We further show that performance can be enhanced by incorporating retriever-specific human guidance and by scaling model size. To facilitate learning over multi-retrieval-step trajectories, we introduce a branching-based rollout technique that improves training stability. Our work provides the first empirical evidence and actionable insights for building truly retriever-aware RAG systems. Code and resources are available at https://github.com/LCO-Embedding/Envs-aware-Information-Retrieval.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM adapts query formulation via reinforcement learning for retrieval
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过强化学习使LLM适应检索查询策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过强化学习使LLM适应检索查询策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过强化学习使LLM适应检索查询策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Explores how LLMs can learn retriever-specific query strategies via RL, revealing distinct optimal styles for different retrievers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前RAG系统忽略了不同检索器需要不同查询策略的问题。本文首次系统分析了如何通过强化学习让LLM学习适配查询策略。实验发现不同检索器存在截然不同的最优查询风格，为检索增强生成提供了新视角。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: using large language models for discovery tasks

quick_skim.title: AGDN: Learning to Solve Traveling Salesman Problem with Anisotropic Graph Diffusion Network
quick_skim.abstract: The Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) is a cornerstone of combinatorial optimization and arises in many practical scenarios. Although graph-based learning approaches have been explored for TSP, the question of how to exploit graph structure more effectively remains open. We present the Anisotropic Graph Diffusion Network (AGDN), a new Graph Neural Network framework designed to solve TSP. Our method tackles two central difficulties: (1) the lack of informative topological prior in fully connected TSP graphs, and (2) losing connected nodes in the optimal solution after the commonly used graph sparsification techniques. To overcome these issues, we construct a MixScore transition matrix that merges node similarity with pairwise distance, and we develop an anisotropic graph diffusion strategy that supports efficient information exchange across multiple hops. Comprehensive experiments spanning diverse instance sizes and node distributions show that AGDN consistently outperforms existing methods while keeping computation time competitive. Furthermore, AGDN generalizes well to problem sizes and distributions beyond those seen during training. The implementation is publicly available at: https://github.com/LabRAI/AGDN.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: graph neural network for solving traveling salesman problem, related to ATSP algorithms
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 图神经网络求解旅行商问题，与ATSP算法相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 图神经网络求解旅行商问题，与ATSP算法相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 图神经网络求解旅行商问题，与ATSP算法相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AGDN uses anisotropic graph diffusion network with MixScore to solve TSP, improving over GNN baselines.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对图学习求解TSP时拓扑先验不足和图稀疏化丢失最优解的问题，本文提出各向异性图扩散网络AGDN。通过构造MixScore转移矩阵融合节点相似性和距离，在多个TSP实例上超越现有GNN模型。该工作为组合优化中的神经网络方法提供了新架构，对ATSP求解有借鉴意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对图学习求解TSP时拓扑先验不足和图稀疏化丢失最优解的问题，本文提出各向异性图扩散网络AGDN。通过构造MixScore转移矩阵融合节点相似性和距离，在多个TSP实例上超越现有GNN模型。该工作为组合优化中的神经网络方法提供了新架构，对ATSP求解有借鉴意义。

quick_skim.title: A Theoretical Framework for Risk Analysis of Stochastic Rankers
quick_skim.abstract: Different from deterministic rankers that seek to maximize relevance at top ranks, stochastic ranking policies instead estimate distributions over permutations, from which rankings are sampled, towards obtaining diversified or fair exposure. Such policies are commonly evaluated in terms of expected effectiveness postreranking. However, the randomness inherent in these policies gives rise to a fundamental but under-explored ex ante question: prior to applying stochastic reranking, how large can the induced variation in retrieval effectiveness be in the worst case? This paper presents a theoretical analysis of reranking risk, defined as the maximum absolute change in discounted cumulative gain (DCG) resulting from a permutation sampled from a stochastic reranking policy applied to a fixed retrieved list.We derive that this risk is governed by the distribution of the recall points in the initial retrieved list. We conduct experiments on submitted runs from the TREC Fairness 2022 track that employ stochastic reranking policies and empirically demonstrate that the effectiveness variations predicted by our theory closely approximate the observed changes in DCG.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: theoretical analysis of stochastic reranking risk
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 随机排序器风险的理论分析
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 随机排序器风险的理论分析
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 随机排序器风险的理论分析
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Analyzes worst-case DCG variation from stochastic reranking, providing theoretical bounds and implications for reranker evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对随机排序策略在重排序后可能引起的检索效果波动问题，提出了重排序风险的理论分析框架，定义了基于折扣累计增益（DCG）最大绝对变化的风险度量，并给出了相应的理论界。研究采用的理论方法分析了随机排序器在worst-case下的表现，为理解随机排序的稳定性提供了数学基础。该工作对重排序器的评估与设计有重要理论指导意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对随机排序策略在重排序后可能引起的检索效果波动问题，提出了重排序风险的理论分析框架，定义了基于折扣累计增益（DCG）最大绝对变化的风险度量，并给出了相应的理论界。研究采用的理论方法分析了随机排序器在worst-case下的表现，为理解随机排序的稳定性提供了数学基础。该工作对重排序器的评估与设计有重要理论指导意义。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 关于reranker训练方法的研究, especially work that connects or combines: reranker training methods; cross encoder training for reranking; pointwise reranker training; listwise reranker training; ranking model training techniques; loss function used in reranker training; how to train a reranker for information retrieval; training methodology of cross encoder reranker; loss functions used in reranker training.

quick_skim.title: VaxjoGNN: A Graph Neural Network for Ontology-Grounded Vaccine Adjuvant Recommendation
quick_skim.abstract: Selecting an effective adjuvant remains a bottleneck in vaccine development, but most computational efforts have targeted antigen discovery rather than adjuvant prioritization. We frame disease-adjuvant matching as a top-k recommendation task on a heterogeneous knowledge graph grounded in biomedical ontologies, integrating curated facts, mechanistic pathways, and textual evidence. We introduce VaxjoGNN, a graph neural network trained with a listwise ranking objective. On a public benchmark, VaxjoGNN achieves NDCG@10 of 0.59 on seen diseases and 0.27 on previously unseen diseases (a 5.4x improvement over a random baseline). The framework provides an ontology-anchored approach to adjuvant prioritization that complements existing antigen-focused tools.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: listwise ranking objective used in GNN training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在GNN训练中使用listwise排序目标
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 在GNN训练中使用listwise排序目标
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 在GNN训练中使用listwise排序目标
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes VaxjoGNN, a GNN trained with listwise ranking objective for vaccine adjuvant recommendation, achieving improved NDCG. The listwise training method is transferable to reranker training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 疫苗佐剂选择是疫苗开发中的瓶颈，现有计算工作多集中于抗原发现。本文提出VaxjoGNN，一个基于生物医学本体异构知识图谱的图神经网络，使用listwise排序目标进行疾病-佐剂匹配的top-k推荐。在公开基准上，对于已知疾病NDCG@10达到0.59，对于未知疾病达到0.27，相比随机基线提升5.4倍。该工作不仅提供了有效的佐剂推荐框架，其listwise训练策略对排序模型训练领域也具有迁移价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 疫苗佐剂选择是疫苗开发中的瓶颈，现有计算工作多集中于抗原发现。本文提出VaxjoGNN，一个基于生物医学本体异构知识图谱的图神经网络，使用listwise排序目标进行疾病-佐剂匹配的top-k推荐。在公开基准上，对于已知疾病NDCG@10达到0.59，对于未知疾病达到0.27，相比随机基线提升5.4倍。该工作不仅提供了有效的佐剂推荐框架，其listwise训练策略对排序模型训练领域也具有迁移价值。
